finding_results.notes: The closing movement now separates evidence from inference. Maren names the observed losses, treats the System's logic as conditional, and holds multiple plausible explanations in play while preserving the chapter's responsibility shift.

Vess's retained-skill proof now uses a fresh assassin-specific detail about when to release a strangling wire, avoiding the prohibited style-bible rib-entry image while keeping her operational competence intact.

The gap reveal no longer cycles through redundant explanatory passes. The mechanistic narrator gloss is gone, the anger setup is compressed, the door metaphor remains the main second formulation, and the old abstract recap is replaced by a sentence about Maren's changed understanding of Vess.

Rebuilt all four cited reveal beats. Vess's sister-name beat (line 127) now shows her attempting phonetic retrieval — trying sounds, stopping, restarting — before landing on a flat operational statement instead of describing the shape of the absence. Vess's anger beat (lines 143, 147) now documents the missing emotion through contractual evidence (risk-factor flag, below-rate fee) rather than the room/door metaphor chain; her landing is one operational sentence ('a reason I can document and can't feel'). Aldric's gap beat (line 207) now shows him attempting to reconstruct what he saw — reaching for 'structures, lines' — failing, then falling back to precise first-chamber data (dimensions, contamination readings) as concrete contrast with the missing second-chamber understanding; abstract landing compressed to 'clean edge, nothing past it.' Aldric's elaboration (line 211) compressed from three thesis sentences to one brief diagnostic ('The edge is too clean. Something was there, and it was taken.'). Voice separation maintained: Vess uses contract terms, risk factors, and operational shorthand; Aldric uses chamber measurements, contamination classifications, and uncontracted formal syntax. No character exceeds one abstract/metaphor sentence in the revised beats.

finding_results.notes: Reworked the repeated negation-correction beats in both revelation scenes into direct declarative or sensory phrasing, especially around Vess's silence, Aldric's second-chamber memory, Maren's System inference, and the closing ethical turn. Kept Aldric's line at chapters/chapter_08.md:211 as a deliberate precision distinction because the degraded-versus-removed memory contrast is character-true and functionally important.

pass_summaries.summary: Revised Chapter 8 to remove the style-bible dialogue leak, compress the repeated gap-language around Vess's missing memory and cut-off anger, and reframe the campfire close so the System's editing logic remains an active hypothesis instead of a solved thesis.

Single finding addressed. Replaced polished thesis-speech in both fragment reveal beats with failed-retrieval attempts and concrete operational/classificatory residue. Vess now tries phonetic sounds for the missing name and cites contract documentation as proof of unfelt anger. Aldric now attempts to reconstruct wall patterns and falls back to precise chamber data he can still access. Extended metaphor chains (room/door, emptied/negative-space) removed; each fragment gets at most one brief abstract landing. Voice separation sharpened through vocabulary domain: Vess stays contractual and operational, Aldric stays classificatory and formal. All must-land beats, state deltas, and open hooks preserved.

Reduced the chapter's recurrent "Not X" emphasis pattern across the flagged passages and replaced the key beats with more direct phrasing while preserving voice, chapter state, and the deliberate technical distinction in Aldric's memory analysis.
